ticketing.title: Ticketing
ticketing.body: Welcome — this is Takuto's first-time setup. Pick where it should read tasks from. You can change any of these settings from the Configuration page later.

provider.title: AI provider
provider.body: Choose the AI that drives your work items, then add your own provider key.

git.title: Git & GitHub
git.body: Set the git branch and remote Takuto works from, then connect a GitHub App or personal token.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.body: Review the flows you can run on each work item. Tweak or re-seed them any time.

repositories.title: Repositories
repositories.body: Add the GitHub repositories Takuto can work in — you'll set up workflows for each in the next step.

polling.note: These settings control how Takuto picks up new work items automatically. They're saved when you continue (the Save button below applies them immediately).

stepTimeout.heading: Step timeout
stepTimeout.label: Timeout (seconds)
stepTimeout.invalid: Step timeout must be a positive number.
stepTimeout.hint: Maximum seconds an agent step may run before it is cancelled. Default 1800 (30 min).

dbNote: Database and dashboard port are not configured in this wizard. Takuto writes a config.toml with the defaults (SQLite, port 8080). Edit that file directly to change them.

ticketing.label: Ticketing system
ticketing.adminOnly: Only an admin can change the deployment's ticketing system. You can still manage your own Jira credential below.

none.label: None
none.hint: No ticketing integration. Start work items manually from the dashboard.

github.label: GitHub
github.hint: Poll open GitHub Issues from the repo's remote. No token here — connect a PAT or GitHub App in the GitHub step.

jira.label: Jira
jira.hint: Poll Jira for To Do tickets. Add your Atlassian site, email, and API token below.

jira.connectedAs: ✓ Connected as {{email}} on {{site}}. Leave the form blank to keep it, or enter a new token to replace it.
jira.encryptedNote: Your Jira credentials are stored encrypted and used only for your own work items.
jira.tokenLinkAria: Open the Atlassian API token page (opens in a new tab)

provider.label: Provider
provider.opencodeHint: OpenCode wires Takuto to your self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoint (LM Studio, Ollama, vLLM, private gateways). To use Anthropic / OpenAI directly, pick the Claude or Codex provider instead.
provider.baseUrlPlaceholderDefault: Leave empty to use the vendor public API
provider.cursorNoUpstream: Cursor CLI does not support custom upstream endpoints
provider.cursorNoUpstreamHint: Cursor CLI does not support custom upstream endpoints.
provider.baseUrlOpencodeHint: Required for OpenCode. Point this at your self-hosted OpenAI-compatible server.
provider.modelPlaceholderDefault: Leave empty for the vendor default
provider.modelOpencodeHint: Required for OpenCode. The model id served by your endpoint (e.g. lmstudio/qwen3-coder).

git.heading: Git settings
git.intro: The branch Takuto checks out for each work item, and the git remote it pushes to.
git.baseBranchHint: The branch work-item branches are cut from. Usually "main" or "master".
git.remoteHint: The git remote Takuto fetches from and pushes branches to.
git.adminOnly: Only an admin can change the deployment's git settings.

app.explainer: A shared GitHub App lets everyone on this deployment talk to GitHub without a personal token. Setting it up is optional — each teammate can instead bring their own personal access token below.
app.setupLinkAria: Read the Takuto GitHub App setup guide (opens in a new tab)

pat.heading: Your personal access token (optional)
pat.intro: Add a PAT so commits, PRs, and review comments on your work items are attributed to you. Skip it to run as the shared GitHub App.

banner.notReady: Takuto is not ready — setup required
banner.legacyRunHint: Run docker compose run --rm -it takuto setup to complete setup, then restart.
banner.askAdminProvider: Ask your admin to change the provider
banner.askAdminGeneric: Ask your admin to fix this
banner.ctaAriaExternal: {{label}} (opens documentation in a new tab) — fix: {{message}}
